-i ${i}.description: Senta substitutes actual URL at stream creation. Always use ${i} placeholder in profiles

-re.description: Reads input at native frame rate. Auto-enable for File and HTTP/HLS to prevent faster-than-real-time consumption

-stream_loop -1.description: Auto-enable for File type. Combine with -re for proper file looping broadcast playout

-fflags +genpts.description: Critical for dirty DVB/IPTV sources. genpts regenerates missing PTS; discardcorrupt drops bad TS packets; nobuffer reduces latency on live inputs

-use_wallclock_as_timestamps 1.description: Replaces broken stream timestamps with wall clock time. Essential for capture devices and contribution feeds with corrupt DTS/PTS

-max_delay.description: Buffer delay for out-of-order UDP/RTP packets. Increase for high-jitter contribution links; reduce for low-latency monitoring

-i /logo.png.description: Second -i argument. Used for overlay filter. Logo file must exist on the FFmpeg server at runtime

-f mpegts.description: mpegts is standard for DVB/IPTV contribution and distribution. null useful for analysis without output

-f hls -hls_time.description: Short segments (2s) for low-latency HLS; longer (6s+) for VOD/catchup. Affects startup time vs. stability tradeoff

-hls_list_size.description: 0 for VOD/catchup TV recording. 3–6 for live IPTV. Larger = more recovery window for stalling clients

-hls_flags.description: delete_segments prevents disk fill on live streams. program_date_time required for DVR/catchup timeline anchoring

-hls_segment_type.description: fmp4 required for HEVC/H.265 HLS (Apple mandate). Also needed for CMAF and Low-Latency HLS (LL-HLS)

-hls_enc / -hls_enc_key.description: AES-128 encryption for IPTV OTT delivery. Lightweight DRM without full DRM stack. Key served via HTTPS

-hls_fmp4_init_filename.description: Name of the initialization segment when using fMP4 HLS. Required for HEVC HLS packaging

-strftime 1.description: Enables -%Y%m%d%H%M%S in output filename pattern. Used for DVR catchup pipelines with time-indexed segments

-avoid_negative_ts.description: Use make_zero for IPTV middleware compatibility. Negative timestamps break many HLS players and IPTV STBs

-copyts.description: Passes through input timestamps unchanged. Critical in relay/transcode chains to prevent timestamp resets breaking downstream decoders

-mpegts_service_id.description: DVB Service ID (SID). Must be unique per multiplex. Headend operators control this to match SI/PSI tables in the mux

-mpegts_pmt_start_pid.description: Starting PID for PMT tables. Control to avoid PID conflicts when multiple services share a mux or remultiplexer

-mpegts_start_pid.description: Starting PID for elementary streams (video, audio). Must be coordinated with mux PID assignment plan

-mpegts_flags.description: initial_discontinuity sets discontinuity flag on startup — prevents STB decoder errors on stream join. pat_pmt_at_frames inserts PSI more frequently

-pcr_period.description: PCR injection interval in the TS. Too infrequent causes STB clock recovery issues. DVB spec recommends ≤100ms; 20–40ms is safe for broadcast

-pes_payload_size.description: Controls PES packet payload size. Affects latency in contribution chains. Smaller = lower latency, more overhead; larger = more efficient

-c:v.description: copy passes stream through without re-encoding. mpeg2video for legacy DVB-SD playout. NVENC/VAAPI for GPU-accelerated transcoding

-hwaccel.description: Auto-set: cuda for NVENC, vaapi for VAAPI. Must appear before -i. Without correct hwaccel, GPU codecs fall back to CPU silently

-preset.description: Controls encode speed vs. quality tradeoff. NVENC ll/llhq presets for low-latency broadcast contribution (sub-100ms)

-profile:v.description: Baseline for maximum STB compatibility. High for quality. main10/high10 for 10-bit HDR pipelines. High422 for broadcast-quality chroma

-level:v.description: Separate from profile. Low-end STBs often require level ≤4.0. Level 4.1 supports up to 1080p50. Incorrect level causes hardware decoder rejection

-pix_fmt.description: yuv420p for maximum decoder compatibility. yuv422p for broadcast-grade chroma (XDCAM-style). 10le variants for HDR/WCG pipelines. Many IPTV STBs reject non-yuv420p

-s.description: 720x576 is DVB-SD PAL standard. 720x480 for NTSC. Always pair with -aspect for non-square pixel SD content

-aspect.description: Critical for DVB SD: 720x576 content can be 4:3 or 16:9 — the DAR flag tells the decoder. Without it, SD content is stretched or letterboxed incorrectly

-r.description: 25fps = PAL/DVB standard. 29.97 = NTSC. 50/60 for HD progressive. Always set explicitly for broadcast — do not rely on source FPS passthrough

-vsync / -fps_mode.description: passthrough for relay (preserves original timing). cfr for broadcast encoding (ensures constant frame rate). vfr for VOD where frames may be irregular

-g.description: Keyframe interval in frames. For broadcast: GOP = framerate (1-second GOP) or 2×framerate (2-second GOP). Larger GOPs improve compression, shorter improve seek/zap time

-keyint_min.description: Minimum distance between keyframes. For CBR broadcast: set equal to GOP to prevent scene-change keyframes breaking CBR predictability. Combine with -sc_threshold 0

-sc_threshold.description: Set to 0 for strict CBR broadcast. Default 40 inserts extra keyframes on scene changes, destroying CBR bitrate predictability. Essential for DVB contribution encoding

-b:v.description: CBR/VBR target. DVB-S2 typical: SD 2–4 Mbps, HD 6–12 Mbps, UHD 20–40 Mbps. Must stay below mux bandwidth allocation

-crf.description: Quality-based VBR. Not suitable for strict broadcast CBR. Useful for VOD/archive encoding where bitrate budget is flexible

-maxrate.description: VBR peak cap. Set to 110–120% of target bitrate for broadcast. Required alongside -bufsize for proper VBR operation

-bufsize.description: HRD buffer size. Controls bitrate variation window. For broadcast: 1–2× maxrate. Larger = smoother but more decoder buffer required

-bf.description: Many DVB hardware decoders (MPEG-2 era STBs) support 0–2 B-frames max. Set to 0 for maximum compatibility. Fewer B-frames also reduce encode latency

-refs.description: Low-end STBs support 1–4 reference frames. High ref counts improve quality but require more decoder memory. Set to 1–2 for legacy STB compatibility

-bsf:v.description: h264_mp4toannexb required when rewrapping H.264 from MP4/fMP4 to MPEG-TS. hevc_mp4toannexb for HEVC fMP4→TS. mpeg4_unpack_bframes for MPEG-4 ASP compatibility

-forced-idr 1.description: Forces all keyframes to be IDR frames. Required when downstream decoders (STBs, IRDs) need clean random-access points. Especially important for NVENC

-deinterlace (yadif).description: DVB SD and some HD content is interlaced. Use mode=1 (field) for broadcast-quality deinterlace preserving 50fps output. yadif:mode=0 for progressive-only output

-field_order.description: Required when encoding interlaced output. DVB SD PAL is typically top-field-first. Mismatch causes comb artifacts. Set explicitly for any interlaced output

-color_primaries.description: Metadata tag for color pipeline. bt709 for HD/FHD broadcast. bt2020 for UHD/HDR. Wrong value causes downstream color processing errors in HbbTV/DVB streams

-color_trc.description: smpte2084 for HDR10. arib-std-b67 for HLG (broadcast-preferred HDR). Must match the actual encoding — tagging bt709 on an HLG stream causes incorrect display

-colorspace.description: YCbCr matrix coefficients. bt709 for HD. bt2020nc (non-constant luminance) for UHD/HDR. DVB/HbbTV compliance requires correct tagging

-c:a.description: ac3/eac3 standard for DVB and cable. aac for HLS/OTT. libopus for WebRTC/low-latency streams. copy for relay without re-encoding

-b:a.description: DVB AC3 typically 192–384k. AAC stereo: 128–192k. EAC3 5.1: 384–640k. Do not set below 96k for broadcast audio

-ar.description: 48000 Hz is the DVB/broadcast standard. 44100 for MP3/legacy. Always resample to 48000 when mixing with broadcast equipment. 96000 for hi-fi archiving

-ac.description: DVB primary audio typically stereo (2). 5.1 for premium/cinema channels. Mono for commentary/secondary audio tracks

-channel_layout.description: Explicit layout beyond channel count. 5.1(side) vs 5.1 affects surround speaker routing. Required for correct surround passthrough on STBs

-dialnorm.description: Embedded in AC3/EAC3 bitstream. Tells Dolby decoder the dialogue level so it can normalize playback volume. Required for broadcast Dolby compliance. -24 is EBU R128 reference level

-af loudnorm.description: Legally required for broadcast in most European markets (EBU R128). Target: I=-23 LUFS, True Peak ≤ -1 dBTP. Enable as audio filter with two-pass for best accuracy

-bsf:a.description: aac_adtstoasc required when packaging AAC-ADTS into MP4/fMP4 containers. Commonly needed when remuxing HLS streams

-map 0:a:0.description: DVB streams carry multiple audio tracks (multi-language). Use -map to select or pass through specific tracks. Currently absent from constructor — high-priority gap for IPTV providers

-metadata:s:a:0.description: Language tag on audio streams. Required for DVB SI compliance and automatic audio track selection on STBs and middleware platforms

-apad.description: Pads audio with silence when audio is shorter than video. Prevents sync loss from intermittent audio dropout common in DVB contribution feeds

-c:s.description: DVB streams carry DVB subtitles (dvbsub) and teletext (dvbteletext). Minimum: expose -sn to drop subtitles. Currently completely absent from constructor

-sn.description: Drops all subtitle streams. Useful when output container does not support subtitles (e.g. plain UDP MPEG-TS without subtitle PIDs)

-map 0:s.description: Pass specific subtitle streams. Required when source has multiple subtitle languages and output must preserve them

-metadata:s:s:0.description: Language metadata on subtitle streams. Required for DVB SI compliance and EPG-driven subtitle selection on STBs

-map.description: Fundamental for multi-audio DVB streams. Without -map, FFmpeg selects "best" stream per type — silently dropping secondary audio/subtitle tracks. High-priority gap

-map_metadata.description: Controls whether input metadata (service name, language tags) is copied to output. Set -1 to strip all metadata for clean output profiles

-map_chapters.description: Chapter markers from source. Usually irrelevant for live IPTV; set -1 for clean TS output

-filter:v yadif.description: yadif mode=1 for field-rate output (25i → 50p). mode=0 for frame-rate output (25i → 25p). Use mode=1 for broadcast-quality output preserving motion

-filter:v scale.description: Auto-applied when Frame Size is not Original. Use -2 to maintain aspect ratio. Pair with setsar=1:1 for non-square pixel sources

-filter:v setsar.description: Must apply after scaling non-square-pixel DVB SD sources. Without setsar=1:1 after rescaling, output appears distorted on square-pixel displays

-filter:v fps.description: Alternative to -r flag. Uses frame interpolation/dropping. Smoother than -r for fractional rates. Prefer over -r when quality matters

-filter:v drawtext.description: Channel bug/watermark as text. Alternative to PNG overlay. Supports dynamic variables: %{localtime}, stream timecode. No second -i required

-filter_complex overlay.description: Position: top-left=10:10, top-right=W-w-10:10, bottom-right=W-w-10:H-h-10. Requires second -i (logo path). Cannot combine with -c:v copy

-filter_complex.description: Used for deinterlace+overlay combination. Editor/passthrough mode only — constructor builds this automatically when both deinterlace and logo are active

-af loudnorm.description: EBU R128 compliance for broadcast. I = integrated loudness (LUFS). TP = true peak (dBTP). LRA = loudness range. Two-pass mode gives best accuracy for pre-recorded content

-af aresample.description: High-quality resampling. soxr resampler preferred for broadcast. Alternative to -ar flag — use when more control over resampling quality is needed

-af channelmap.description: Remaps audio channels. Useful for extracting specific channels from multi-channel DVB audio, e.g. extracting dialogue track from 5.1 to stereo

-af apad.description: Pads audio with silence to match video duration. Prevents A/V sync loss when audio has gaps. Common issue in DVB contribution from satellite uplink feeds

${varname}.description: Placeholder in command template. Operators set values at stream creation. Enables reusable profiles parameterized per channel

Command Template.description: The ffmpeg flag with $value replaced at runtime. Template is emitted verbatim into the command. Multiple flags supported: -maxrate $value -bufsize ${value}x2

Default Value.description: Pre-filled when operator creates a stream. Leaves operator an educated starting point. Use sensible broadcast defaults

-y.description: Always included. Prevents FFmpeg blocking on output file overwrite prompt in unattended daemon operation

-hide_banner.description: Suppresses FFmpeg version/build info from log output. Always included for clean Senta log parsing

-loglevel.description: Controls FFmpeg log verbosity. warning for production. info/verbose for debugging. Piped to Senta log subsystem

-threads.description: Thread count for CPU encoding. 0 = auto (uses all available cores). Set explicitly on shared servers to limit resource usage per stream

validValues: Time in seconds or HH:MM:SS format
-t.description: Stops encoding after specified duration. Useful for test transcodes, clip extraction, or scheduled programme recording windows

validValues: Time in seconds or HH:MM:SS format
-ss.description: Seek to position before processing. Use before -i for fast seek (keyframe-accurate) or after -i for slow seek (frame-accurate). Relevant for VOD clip extraction

-stats.description: Prints encoding progress (fps, bitrate, time) to stderr. Always enable for Senta monitoring integration. Parse for real-time quality metrics

-progress.description: Machine-readable progress output. Senta can parse for real-time monitoring. Format: key=value pairs at each stats interval
